title: Warm Earthy Greek Lamb Flatbread
description: A show-off Mediterranean dish featuring earthy flavors and velvety texture, made with strong white bread flour and FODMAP-friendly ingredients. This dish is perfect for a special occasion or dinner party.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the strong white bread flour, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add in the garlic-infused oil and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rest for 1 hour in a warm place.
Roll out the dough into a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Top the dough with the diced lamb, kalamata olives, artichoke hearts, and crumbly feta cheese.
Sprinkle the chopped fresh oregano over the top of the flatbread.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
